name: Microsoft Azure
description: Microsoft Azure is the second-largest public cloud provider, offering 200+ services across 60+ regions. Azure benefits from deep integration with Microsoft enterprise products (Entra ID, Microsoft 365, Defender) and provides a comprehensive shared responsibility model aligned with industry standards.

securityModel.name: Azure Shared Responsibility Model
securityModel.description: Microsoft secures the physical infrastructure, network, and host layers. Responsibility shifts to the customer progressively from IaaS (most customer responsibility) through PaaS to SaaS (most Microsoft responsibility). Identity and access management, data, and endpoint protection are always shared or customer responsibilities.

layers.scope: Physical datacenter security, network infrastructure, host infrastructure, hypervisor, operating system (for PaaS/SaaS)
Identity and directory, applications, network controls, OS (for IaaS), data classification and encryption, endpoint protection

frameworks.name: CIS Microsoft Azure Foundations Benchmark
frameworks.description: Consensus-based security configuration guidelines for Azure subscriptions. Covers identity and access, Microsoft Defender, storage, database, logging and monitoring, networking, VMs, and key management. Published by the Center for Internet Security.

frameworks.name: Azure Well-Architected Framework - Security Pillar
frameworks.description: Microsoft-authored best practices for securing Azure workloads. Organized around five design principles: plan resources and operations for security, design to protect confidentiality, design to protect integrity, design to protect availability, and sustain and evolve security posture.

frameworks.name: Microsoft Cloud Security Benchmark (MCSB)
frameworks.description: Microsoft's unified security baseline for Azure, AWS, and GCP. Provides detailed control mappings to CIS, NIST 800-53, and PCI DSS. Serves as the default standard in Microsoft Defender for Cloud for compliance monitoring.

malaysiaContext.notes: Azure does not have a dedicated Malaysia region. The nearest region is Southeast Asia (Singapore). Azure Malaysia is a planned future region. For PDPA 2010 data residency considerations, evaluate Singapore hosting or explore Azure Confidential Computing for additional data protection guarantees. Azure Government (US) is not applicable for Malaysian organizations.
